mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the threshold over a 3-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of triggered cases across rolling 5-year windows, invalidating the demand synchronization premise.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which predicts positive short-term S&P 500 momentum during economic expansions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when industrial production increases during expansion phases over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods following inflation threshold breaches, contradicting the valuation compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of observations over a 4-year backtest, refuting the earnings growth linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad economic growth that directly boosts corporate revenues, driving positive short-term S&P 500 price appreciation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDP growth is positive, breaking the revenue transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal manufacturing contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative equity market returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >50% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines over a 3-year test.
